title: Call of Duty
edition_pinned: Call of Duty 4: Modern Warfare (Infinity Ward, 2007) — design-canonical multiplayer: create-a-class, XP unlocks, Prestige, killstreaks (3/5/7). Later scorestreaks/specialists are descendants, not the pin.
moat: Killstreaks convert a no-death kill chain into map-scale power while XP unlocks reshape the next loadout.

loop_skeleton.description: Spawn with a create-a-class loadout, fight short lives in a mode map, chain kills without dying to earn UAV/airstrike/helicopter streaks, die and reset the streak, bank match XP to unlock weapons, attachments, perks, and Prestige resets.

depth_mechanisms.name: killstreak ladder
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Three kills without dying call a UAV, five an airstrike, seven a helicopter. Death zeroes streak progress. Designers replaced zone-based air support with streaks so deathmatch flow was not broken by map trigger camping.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If killstreak rewards are removed, average mid-match power spikes fall and high-skill players lose a primary reason to risk extended lives instead of passive play.

depth_mechanisms.name: create-a-class perk loadout
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Custom classes pick primary, sidearm, attachments, special grenade, and three perk tiers. Loadout is chosen before the match so weapon hunting mid-map is not required.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If players cannot preset classes and must loot weapons only on the map, time-to-meaningful-firefight rises and perk-based playstyle identity disappears.

depth_mechanisms.name: xp prestige treadmill
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Match actions grant XP that ranks the player and unlocks weapons/attachments/camos. Hitting the cap enables Prestige: reset rank for an insignia and a new climb (up to ten on consoles).
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If XP unlocks and Prestige are removed and all weapons are free at rank 1, second-session goals collapse to raw K/D with no loadout ladder.

feel_signature: A life is a short commit from spawn; each kill without dying ticks a streak meter toward a map-scale reward; death zeroes the meter and returns the player to spawn with the same class.
second_session_answer: Players return to finish the next weapon unlock or Prestige rank and to practice maps/classes — goals that persist outside any single match outcome.
packaging_lessons: Ship deathmatch-readable maps and preset classes before mode bloat; COD4 maps were built around deathmatch layouts first.
Put mid-match power on no-death chains (killstreaks), not on map pickups that stall flow.
anti_lessons: Do not copy killstreaks into a PvE campaign without redesign — the mechanism assumes respawn-based multiplayer opponents.
Do not layer pay-to-unlock weapons onto the XP ladder if the design goal is skill-and-time progression (later remaster microtransactions are a negative lesson).

system_bom.note: spawn-fight-die cycle is the match atom
modes and maps are the required multiplayer surface
weapons, attachments, perks, camos unlock by rank
Prestige resets rank for long-horizon goals
death resets streak but not XP; match score is separate from personal unlock goals
create-a-class and streak feedback must be readable under 60fps combat
K/D and rank comparison sustain social return

citations.claim: moat/killstreak ladder: 3-kill UAV, 5-kill airstrike, 7-kill helicopter; death resets streak progress.
depth_mechanisms.killstreak origin: team replaced zone air-support triggers with killstreaks because zone design hurt deathmatch flow.
depth_mechanisms.create-a-class perk loadout: primary, sidearm, attachments, special grenade, three perks; preset before match.
depth_mechanisms.xp prestige treadmill: XP unlocks content; Prestige resets rank for insignia up to ten times.
feel_signature / packaging: multiplayer XP leveling, Prestige, create-a-class, perks, killstreaks became series hallmarks.
system_bom postmortem context: Infinity Ward multiplayer design priorities and development notes.
edition pin justification: COD4 introduced the modern CoD multiplayer template later titles iterate.
